taskDescription: Click on all the bones and bony landmarks of the lower limb mentioned in the text below.
textField: The hip bone is formed by fusion of the *ilium*, *ischium*, and *pubis*, which all meet at the *acetabulum*. The *femur* is the longest and strongest bone in the body. Its head sits in the acetabulum, and the *fovea* is a pit on the head for the ligament. The greater and lesser *trochanters* are important muscle attachment sites. Below the knee, the *tibia* carries body weight while the *fibula* does not.
